records.ety: [Pref. im- not + provident: cf. L. improvidus. See Provident, and cf. Imprudent.]

defs: Not provident; wanting foresight or forethought; not foreseeing or providing for the future; negligent; thoughtless; as, an improvident man.
quotes: Improvident soldiers! had your watch been good, This sudden mischief never could have fallen.
